zh.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 英國藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓
zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Petronella de Banastre於1312年出生在英國藍卡爾布利黑頓。她於大約1333年與Sir Adam de Banastre在英國藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1388年5月4日在英國藍卡爾可勞頓享年76歲。

zh-hans.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 英国藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Petronella de Banastre于1312年出生在英国藍卡爾布利黑頓。她于大约1333年与Sir Adam de Banastre在英国藍卡爾班可 霍爾 亞爾 普雷斯頓结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1388年5月4日在英国藍卡爾可勞頓享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Petronella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Fedele, Francesco, Marco, Salvatore, Vito.
Italian (southern): from Petronella, a feminine pet form of Petrone or Petronio .

variants.name: Petrone
meanings: From Latin Petronilla, originally a feminine diminutive of the Roman family name Petronius (of uncertain derivation). The name Petronilla was borne by a 1st-century martyr, and early in the Christian era came to be connected with Peter , so that in many legends surrounding her she is described as a companion or even the daughter of St Peter .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Petronella de Banastre was born in 1312, in Bretherton, Lancashire, England. She married Sir Adam de Banastre about 1333, in Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 4 May 1388, in Claughton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 76.

Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 4 mai 1388, à Claughton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans.

ko.Bank Hall near Preston, Lancashire, Engl: 영국 랭카실르 뱅크 홀 니아어 플레스턴
ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Petronella de Banastre(은)는 1312년 영국 랭카실르 브레더턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1333년 영국 랭카실르 뱅크 홀 니아어 플레스턴에서 Sir Adam de Banastre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1388년 5월 4일 영국 랭카실르 클로그h턴에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Petronella de Banastre родилась в 1312 г. в местности бретиртон, ланкашир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Adam de Banastre около 1333 г. в местности банк холл нир престон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 4 мая 1388 г. в местности клотон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 76 лет.
